data.title: Mid-to-Low cap consumer staples stocks facing poor EPS revision grades
data.description:
As the earnings week begins, a review of mid to low cap consumer staples stocks reveals several companies currently receiving the weakest grades based on analyst earnings revisions.

This analysis focuses on identifying stocks where analysts have significantly lowered their earnings per share expectations.

The metric used for this assessment is the EPS Revisions Grade, which reflects the direction and magnitude of analyst estimate changes. The following list contains consumer staples companies that have received grades of ‘F’ or ‘D-’, indicating substantial downward revisions to earnings expectations.

Here are the consumer staples stocks with the lowest EPS Revisions Grades:

  1. Sendas Distribuidora S.A. (ASAIY) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  2. Edgewell Personal Care (EPC) — EPS Revisions Grade: F 
  3. Flowers Foods (FLO) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  4. Premium Brands (PRBZF) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  5. PriceSmart (PSMT) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  6. Shiseido Company (SSDOY) — EPS Revisions Grade: F 
  7. USANA Health Sciences, Inc. (USNA) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  8. Westrock Coffee Company (WEST) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  9. BellRing Brands, Inc. (BRBR) — EPS Revisions Grade: D-
  10. Barry Callebaut AG (BRRLY) — EPS Revisions Grade: D-

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS)

data.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer says X working to comply with UK law on AI deepfakes
data.description:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Wednesday that the X social network is working to comply with British law pertaining to artificial intelligence deepfake images. 

“I have been informed this morning that X is acting to ensure full compliance with UK law,” Starmer told parliament, according to Reuters. 

Formerly known as Twitter, X merged with xAI (X.AI) last year. Elon Musk controls both companies.

The U.K. is set to bring into force a law — perhaps as soon as this week — that will make it illegal for companies to provide tools for artificial intelligence deepfakes, according to previous reporting. Technology Secretary Liz Kendall told the House of Commons that the law would prevent the tools from being released. She also said the AI deepfakes were not “harmless,” but rather “weapons of abuse.”

Concerns have risen recently in the country regarding x.AI's Grok chatbot, following a growing outcry over its use for sexualized deepfakes of women and children. On Friday, xAI said it restricted the use of AI service Grok's image generation features to paying subscribers.

In response to the outrage, Musk took to X on Jan. 3 and wrote that “anyone using Grok to make illegal content will suffer the same consequences as if they upload illegal content.”

On Monday, the U.K. online safety watchdog Ofcom launched an investigation into Musk's X to determine if the company complied with its duties to protect people in the U.K. from content that is illegal in the country.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Fed's Paulson says she still sees room for more rate cuts
data.description:
Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Anna Paulson said Wednesday she still feels "cautiously optimistic on inflation" after the latest data, reiterating her view for additional interest-rate cuts later this year.

“I am feeling cautiously optimistic on inflation, and I see a decent chance that we will end the year with inflation that is close to 2% on a run-rate basis,” Paulson said in a prepared speech at an event with the Chamber of Commerce for Greater Philadelphia.

Her overall 2026 economic outlook includes inflation moderating, the labor market stabilizing and around 2% growth. "If all of that happens, then some modest further adjustments to the funds rate would likely be appropriate later in the year,” Paulson said.

On Tuesday, the Bureau of Labor Statistics published the Consumer Price Index report, which featured slightly softer-than-expected core CPI at +2.6% Y/Y in December. Meanwhile, November wholesale inflation data out Wednesday heated up a bit.

Paulson said she will keep a close eye on labor-market developments, as job-market "risks have risen and that has been an important factor in my support for the 75 basis points of cuts that the FOMC did last year. The median Fed official expects only one 25-basis-point reduction this year.

data.title: Clover Health rises as Medicare Advantage enrollments climb 53%
data.description:
Shares of Clover Health Investments (CLOV) traded ~11% higher on Wednesday after the company disclosed that enrollees in its Medicare Advantage ((MA)) plans rose 53% for the 2026 plan year.

As the Annual Enrollment Period came to an end, Clover (CLOV) entered 2026 with roughly 153K members as of Jan. 1, with more than 97% of its MA membership enrolled in its flagship PPO plan, the Wilmington, Delaware-based managed care firm added.

Additionally, the company said that it expects to achieve its first-ever GAAP net income profitability this year thanks to cost optimization exercises and other industry-specific factors such as the 2026 CMS final rate update.

“Together, we believe these dynamics will create compounding earnings and margin expansion over time, which we expect to position Clover to deliver its first year of GAAP net income profitability in 2026,” CEO Andrew Toy remarked.

data.title: Fed's Powell gets top approval rating among Washington leaders
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is the target of a Justice Department investigation into his handling of the refurbishment of the Fed's headquarters.

Powell has said that the investigation is motivated by the White House's opposition to FOMC rate policy, something that the president and administration deny.

Looking at a December Gallup poll, Powell has the highest approval rating (44%) among select Washington officials, including President Donald Trump, Attorney General Pam Bondi, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Democratic and Republican Congressional leaders.

data.title: Robotic software startup Skild AI secures $1.4B in latest funding round: report
data.description:
Skild AI, a startup focused on building software for robotic learning, has secured $1.4B in its latest Series C funding round, propelling its valuation to $14B, according to Bloomberg.

The round was led by SoftBank Group (SFTBF)(SFTBY) and included participation from Nvidia (NVDA), Bezos Expeditions, which is Amazon (AMZN) founder Jeff Bezos' venture capital firm, and Macquarie Group (MCQEF), the report said. Other investors were Lightspeed Venture Partners, Felicis Ventures, Sequoia Capital, Coatue Management, Samsung (SSNLF) and Salesforce (CRM). 

Skild AI is attempting to build a software model that allows a robot to complete a wide range of tasks, or general-purpose robotics, without the need for human operators to guide the robot. The company is training the software through visual learning.

"Humans don't learn to make tea by being told the exact Newton-meters of force to apply to a kettle," the company said. "We learn through observational learning. We possess a foundation of kinematics and dynamics that allows us to watch a visual demonstration, internalize the intent, and map those actions onto our own bodies. This data—human video—is already abundant. From first-person 'egocentric' headcam footage to the millions of instructional videos on YouTube, the 'internet-scale' dataset for robotics already exists. It just isn't 'robot-native.'"

The Pittsburgh-based company was founded in 2023.

data.title: Existing home sales rise more than expected in December
data.description:
December Existing Home Sales: +5.1% M/M to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.35M vs. 4.230M consensus and 4.14M prior (revised from 4.13M), according to data released by the National Association of Realtors on Thursday.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Business inventories rise past consensus in October
data.description:
Business inventories in October 2025: +0.3% vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.3% in September (revised from +0.2%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Wednesday.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: CNH Industrial rises to 3-mth high on Goldman Sachs upgrade
data.description:
Shares of CNH Industrial (CNH) climbed to touch a 3-month high on Wednesday after Goldman Sachs upgraded the agricultural and construction equipment maker, pointing to early signs that industry conditions may be stabilizing.

Goldman analyst Daniela Costa raised her recommendation on CNH Industrial (CNH) to Buy from Neutral, saying there is growing evidence that end markets and company earnings are nearing a cyclical low. The upgrade helped lift CNH (CNH) shares about 2.7% in first half hour of trading in New York.

Costa said improving trends in demand and inventory suggest the downturn in agricultural equipment markets may be easing, supporting a more constructive outlook for the company’s performance.

CNH's (CNH) product brands include Case IH, New Holland, Steyr and Iveco.
